cross_scene_arcs.title: The Reluctant Return and Reclamation of Power
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: McGinty transitions from being a retired coach summoned under crisis to an empowered architect of a new team identity, reclaiming control through firm conditions and decisive personnel choices.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: McGinty is out of coaching, living in retirement, and initially resistant to reentry.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He establishes full authority over team direction, enforcing his vision with unconventional player selections.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His internal conflict about leaving retirement remains, raising questions about long-term commitment.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship and Fracture: The McGinty-Falco Relationship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: McGinty builds a deep mentor-mentee bond with Shane Falco, guiding his redemption arc through psychological insight and leadership, only to ultimately reject him after a moral failure, marking a tragic rupture.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shane is isolated and resistant; McGinty initiates contact to reignite his ambition.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: McGinty turns his back on Shane, severing their relationship after a breakdown in integrity during the final game.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional rift is left unhealed, with no path to reconciliation or redemption for Shane.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Vision vs. Tradition: Clashing Coaching Philosophies
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: McGinty's unorthodox approach to team building—centered on overlooked talent and cultural inclusivity—sparks ongoing resistance from staff and systemic skepticism, culminating in a definitive break from traditional roster policies.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Coaching staff adheres to conventional metrics and strategies for player selection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: McGinty institutionalizes his vision by banning returning union players and fully committing to the replacement roster.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Staff skepticism and media scrutiny threaten the sustainability of his philosophy under public pressure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Legacy and Self-Justification
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: McGinty's return is framed as both a redemptive mission and a potential act of ego, with his domestic stability and strategic control elevating his status, even as doubts emerge about his true motivations.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: McGinty is viewed as a retired legend, physically and emotionally distant from the team's chaos.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He secures long-term influence over the team's composition and legacy, positioning himself as a transformative figure beyond the strike.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether his actions stem from altruism or self-validation remains ambiguous, potentially undermining his moral authority.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Crisis to Moral Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As the team faces collapse, McGinty evolves from a tactical coach into a moral leader, reframing failure as dignity and victory as integrity, culminating in a legacy-defining speech that transcends winning.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The team is in disarray, accumulating penalties and losing cohesion under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: McGinty redefines success as self-respect and emotional honor, galvanizing the team for a final stand rooted in principle.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term impact of this moral reframe on players' futures and the owner's redemption remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of Legacy and the Craving for Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: O'Neil transitions from a distant legend symbolized by a stadium to an active, desperate force trying to reclaim control over the team's fate as his physical and mental health decline. His status shifts from revered icon to fragile autocrat defying institutional rules to fulfill a dying wish.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: O'Neil is a legendary figure whose presence is felt through the stadium's nickname, 'The Big O,' though he is physically absent.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: O'Neil asserts absolute control over team decisions, defying union agreements and demanding victory as his final legacy, even as legal consequences loom.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The conflict between O'Neil's personal will and institutional obligations remains unresolved, with the threat of stadium closure if union players are not reinstated.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emotional Dependence and Psychological Decline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: O'Neil's emotional volatility, substance dependency, and deteriorating dignity reveal an internal battle between pride and helplessness, exacerbated by his reliance on sedatives and food as symbols of lost autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: O'Neil suffers an emotional breakdown after a Redskins loss and is sedated by his nurse, exposing his psychological fragility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Reduced to eating pureed food, O'Neil pleads for a bite of steak, underscoring his eroded dignity and psychological distress.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: O'Neil's addiction and emotional instability remain unaddressed, suggesting a deeper personal crisis that undermines his authority and humanity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rebuilding the Team Through Authority and Influence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: O'Neil reasserts his authority by shaping the team's leadership and personnel decisions, from selecting Shane Falco to demanding McGinty's return and leveraging political connections to fill critical roles.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: O'Neil evaluates Shane Falco via voiceover, positioning himself as a discerning authority on talent.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: O'Neil secures McGinty's return and intervenes to resolve the safety shortage using political influence, cementing his role as the team's de facto architect.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tensions persist between O'Neil's vision and McGinty's autonomy, and the team still lacks key players, raising doubts about long-term viability.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Despair to Reinvigoration Through Victory
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: O'Neil's emotional state swings from devastating grief after a loss to renewed vitality following the team's climactic win, illustrating how his identity is inextricably tied to the team's success.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: O'Neil experiences uncontrollable grief after a Redskins loss, requiring sedation to regain composure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He celebrates joyfully after the team's victory, emotionally reconnected and reinvigorated.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His emotional dependence on the team's performance remains unaddressed, leaving his stability vulnerable to future outcomes.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Reluctant Return: From Retirement to Reawakening
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Shane transitions from emotional detachment and self-imposed exile to a reluctant re-engagement with football, triggered by subconscious memories and culminating in his acceptance of a return to professional play.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shane has resigned himself to life after football, emotionally disengaged and working in isolation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Shane commits to rejoining the Washington Redskins as a replacement player, reigniting his football identity under pressure and public scrutiny.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Shane has not yet reconciled his fear of failure and physical pain with his latent desire to reclaim his talent and purpose through football.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Identity Reclaimed: From Self-Doubt to Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Shane evolves from an insecure replacement player struggling with identity and authority to a recognized team leader, validated by Coach McGinty and embraced by the team despite ongoing physical and emotional challenges.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shane lacks confidence in his role, symbolized by being outfitted with the wrong helmet and hesitating in self-identification.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Shane is officially confirmed as a permanent team member and emotional leader, trusted by McGinty to carry the team forward.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Shane suppresses visible physical pain, leaving his long-term resilience and ability to perform under pressure uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Love as Redemption: Emotional Isolation to Vulnerability
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Shane moves from self-destructive isolation to emotional openness through his relationship with Annabelle, who becomes a moral anchor, enabling him to confront trauma and embrace vulnerability as a source of strength.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shane seeks self-sabotage, inviting exploitation and collapsing emotionally after a game.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Shane publicly affirms his love for Annabelle, integrating emotional maturity with leadership and personal redemption.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Shane's fear of emotional closeness and potential for future hurt remains unaddressed, and his defiance may provoke backlash.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Crucible of Pressure: Survival to Triumph Under Duress
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Shane endures relentless physical and psychological pressure from opponents and internal team chaos, ultimately overcoming domination through tactical ingenuity, resilience, and sacrifice, turning moments of failure into victory.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shane is physically overwhelmed and mentally strained by the Giants' defense, particularly Hank Morris, and is unable to execute under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Shane leads critical scoring plays despite injury, including a touchdown pass under duress, proving his ability to perform in the most extreme conditions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Shane is carried off unconscious, raising serious concerns about his physical condition and ability to continue playing.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Redemption Through Sacrifice: Failure, Guilt, and Unity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After a devastating personal and team failure marked by Lamont's injury, Shane transforms guilt into resolve, unifying the team through public commitment and emotional honesty, ultimately leading them toward redemption on the field.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shane is emotionally shattered, burdened by guilt over Lamont's injury and feeling abandoned by Coach McGinty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Shane rallies the team, reconciles with Annabelle, and leads a successful offensive drive, symbolizing his full emergence as a mature, unified leader.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Pacifico's lingering silence and the unresolved tension with Martel suggest that full team cohesion and acceptance may still be fragile.
